headline: $10,000 to Invest? Coca-Cola or Johnson & Johnson- One Stock Is More Reliable
summary: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) and Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO) both closed out 2025 with Q4 earnings and have raised dividends for 63 consecutive years. But beneath that shared milestone sit two different growth engines, two different risk profiles, and two different answers to what “reliable” means for investors today. Oncology Carries J&J. Zero Sugar Carries Coke. Johnson ... $10,000 to Invest? Coca-Cola or Johnson & Johnson- One Stock Is More Reliable

headline: The Exact Portfolio Size Required to Live Entirely Off Dividends Instead of Your $60,000 Paycheck
summary: It can be an appealing idea to replace your paycheck with passive income. While it sounds exciting on the surface, it is a slightly complicated task. You’ll have to work backwards and find out how much income you need and then divide it with a yield. This will give you an investment amount, based on ... The Exact Portfolio Size Required to Live Entirely Off Dividends Instead of Your $60,000 Paycheck

headline: Can Coca-Cola Balance Pricing, Affordability in Soft Macro Backdrop?
summary: KO shifts from pricing-led growth to volume recovery as affordability pressures rise, testing its ability to sustain demand in a softening macro environment.

headline: Is The Coca-Cola Company (KO) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
summary: Is KO a good stock to buy? We came across a bullish thesis on The Coca-Cola Company on r/dividends by IWantToPlayGame. In this article, we will summarize the bulls’ thesis on KO. The Coca-Cola Company’s share was trading at $75.71 as of March 27th. KO’s trailing and forward P/E were 24.59 and 23.09 respectively according to Yahoo Finance. The Coca-Cola Company, […]

headline: Safe Stocks Exist, You're Just Looking In The Wrong Places
summary: When markets get ugly, most investors start asking the same question: where do I hide? That usually leads them to the wrong answer.
